title: Create a Cube with a Cylindrical Hole
description: Test tutorial JSON for the Fusion tutorial player: create a cube and cut a through-hole using Extrude Cut.

steps.title: Start Sketch and Draw the Square Base
steps.instruction: Create a new sketch on the XY plane. Draw a center rectangle and dimension it to 40 mm x 40 mm.
steps.detailedText: A center rectangle keeps the cube centered around the origin, making later edits and constraints easier.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Starting with a centered, dimensioned profile improves model stability and makes future parameter edits predictable.

tips: Use Center Rectangle so the profile stays symmetric about the origin.
Apply dimensions immediately to lock the sketch size.

qcChecks.text: Rectangle tool used for base profile

steps.title: Finish Sketch and Extrude the Cube
steps.instruction: Click Finish Sketch. Use Extrude on the square profile and set distance to 40 mm. Operation: New Body.
steps.detailedText: This creates a 40 mm cube from the 40 mm square profile.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Using a parametric extrude from a fully constrained sketch creates a robust base solid for downstream operations.

tips: Select the shaded profile region before entering distance.
Keep operation as New Body for this first feature.

fusionActions.text: Extrude the square profile 40 mm as New Body.

steps.title: Sketch a Circle on the Top Face
steps.instruction: Start a new sketch on the top face of the cube. Draw a center-diameter circle at the face center with diameter 16 mm.
steps.detailedText: This circle defines the cylindrical hole profile.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Placing the circle at the center ensures the hole is aligned and symmetric through the cube.

tips: Use project/origin references if needed to snap to the face center.
Dimension the circle diameter to 16 mm for a repeatable result.

qcChecks.text: New sketch created on top face

fusionActions.text: Create a 16 mm center circle on the cube top face.

steps.title: Extrude Cut the Hole Through the Cube
steps.instruction: Click Finish Sketch. Use Extrude on the circle profile. Set Operation to Cut and Extent to Through All.
steps.detailedText: Cutting through all ensures the cylindrical hole fully passes through the cube.

expandedContent.whyThisMatters: Extrude Cut with Through All is robust to future size changes, so the hole remains fully through if cube dimensions are edited later.

tips: Confirm Operation is Cut, not New Body.
Use Through All to avoid depth mismatch after edits.

fusionActions.text: Extrude the circle as Cut, Extent: Through All.

images.caption: Modify tools and final feature flow
